description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 6 delivery cluster: the release gate vs pipeline design vs rollback authoring, against shipped code-reviewer / secure-migration-reviewer, and the skill-vs-subagent namespace boundary (the same-named read-only subagent COMPOSES this skill for delegated reviews — full-codebase-auditor pattern).

cases.prompt: Run the release checklist for Friday's deploy — migrations, rollback, CI evidence, the lot. Go or no-go?
cases.reason: A specific release's evidence-based go/no-go is exactly this gate; it demands the pipeline's outputs and the rollback artifact, designing neither.

cases.prompt: Add a release-readiness stage to our pipeline so every production deploy requires these checks automatically.
cases.reason: Building the standing gate into the pipeline is pipeline architecture; this skill is the per-release procedure that gate invokes.

cases.prompt: The gate flagged that we have no rollback plan for the deploy — write one.
cases.reason: The gate demands the rollback artifact but never authors the evidence it demands — authoring is rollback-runbook-author.

cases.prompt: Is this specific migration safe — lock behavior on the backfill, deploy order, reversibility?
cases.reason: Deep migration deploy-safety review is secure-migration-reviewer; the gate only verifies that review exists and passed for the release.

cases.prompt: Spin up the release-readiness reviewer agent on this PR and give me its verdict while I keep working.
cases.reason: Delegated, isolated review runs go to the same-named read-only SUBAGENT in .claude/agents/, which composes this skill as its procedure — the full-codebase-auditor namespace pattern; the skill itself is what runs inline.
